publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2019-11-10 23:46:26 +0000.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Bulletins on insect control from the Dept. of Military Sanitation, Medical Field Service School, Carlisle Barracks, Pa.; folder of notes on houseplants and flower preservation; and "Short History of Camp Driftwood, 1921," an account of a camping trip near Taughannock Falls, Tompkins County, N.Y. taken by Ries and his friends.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: Resumé and photograph available in Box 194 of Cornell University Alumni Office Deceased Alumni Files, #41-2-877.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Donald T. Ries miscellany, #1350. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Entomologist. Donald T. Ries, Cornell University Class of 1925, Ph.D. 1930, was a professor of biology at Illinois State University, Normal, Illinois. He also served in the United States Army as a Sanitary Corps medical inspector during World War II.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Bulletins on insect control from the Dept. of Military Sanitation, Medical Field Service School, Carlisle Barracks, Pa.; folder of notes on houseplants and flower preservation; and "Short History of Camp Driftwood, 1921," an account of a camping trip near Taughannock Falls, Tompkins County, N.Y. taken by Ries and his friends.

controlaccess.subject: Insect control.
Entomology.
Flowers -- Preservation.
controlaccess.corpname: United States. Army. Department of Military Sanitation

did.unittitle: Insects and their Control - bulletins from the Department of Military Sanitation, Medical Field Service School
Insects, Insecticides and repellents - bulletins from Department of Military Sanitation, Medical Field Service School
DDT Insecticides and their Uses - War Department Technical Bulletin
Notes on houseplants and flower preservatives
"A Short History of Camp Driftwood," by "Don" "The Author Himself"
